California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
April 23, 1990
Dorothy S. Maron
Dorothy S. lilaron Re-Election committee
P. o. Box 5306
oxnard, cA 93o31
RE: Your Request for
Idformal Assistance
Our Fil-e No. I-9 0-2 02
Dear Ms. Maron:
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the PoIitical 
Reform Act (the rrActrr).r Your ques-
tions are hypothetical or general in nature, therefore we consider
your request to be for inforrnal assistance pursuant to Regulation
raezs 1cf (copy enclosed).2
OUESTIONS
1. 
You have a committee established for your city council
seat. If you decide to run for mayor, are you required to
establish a separate connittee?
2. 
Dtay you transfer funds from your city council committee
to your committee for mayor?
3. 
How do you handle contributions received by you when the
contributor does not specify which conrmittee they are intended
for?
1 
co.r"..rment code Sections 81OOO-9L015. AII statutory refer-
ences are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et ses. A1l. references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 InforrnaL assistance d.oes not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Sec-
tion 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3). )
'{28 J street' suite 8oo ' p.o' Box 8o7 o Sacranrento cA 958o4-ogo7 o (916) 3zz-566t\


A-90-202
Page 2
4. 
rf
may you run
5. 
As
have in your
funds under
you are the subject of a recal-l as city councilwoman,
for mayor at the sane time?
the subject of a recall, can you use the funds you
current committee? can you raise and spend defense
your current city council committee ID number?
CONCLUSIONS
1. 
You must establish a separate committee and bank account
to run for mayor.
2. 
You may transfer funds betw6en your own controlled com-
nittees .
3. 
When contributions received are not designated by the
contributor for a particular controll-ed cornmittee, you rnay a1--
locate the contribution to any of your controlled committees.
4. 
The Act does not address the issue of the ability of an
officeholder who is the subject of a recaIl effort to seek elec-
tion to another office. 
you should contact the Secretary of
State, Elections Division or a private attorney regardinA this
natter.
5. 
You may use contributions received in
previous election to the same office to oppose
contributions received into this account would
contribution limits irnposed by proposition 73.
ANALYSIS
.The Act, as arnended by proposition 73, requires that a
candidate must estabr,ish one campaign bank'acc6unt for each of-
fice. 
(section 85201.) section aaior reguires candidatei -to rire
a statenent of organization within 10 dayj after receiving
contributions totaring $1,ooo or more in a calendar year. comrnis-
sion regurations further require the esiabristrneni oi-i-=.pir"t"
controlled committee for eaCh campaign bank account:
When a candidate is required to file a statenent of
organization for a controll6d cornrnittee pursuant to Co,r"rn_
nent code Section 84101, the candidate slra1l estab].ish a
separate controlled committee for each specific "faia;
identified in statements filed UV ine ".-r.,aiaai. p"i="unt t"
Government Code Section 85200.
connection with a
the reca1I. The
be subject to the
Regulation 1852I (copy enclosed)


A-90-202
Page 3
Regarding transfers betsreen a candidate,s controlled commit-
tees, on May 15, 1999, the United States District Court, Eastern
District of Catifornia enjoined the Commission from enforcing that
portion of Section 85304 \.rhich prohibited a candidate or of-
ficeholder from transferring funds among his or her or./n controlred
committees. (SEIU, AFL-CIO, CLC, et aI. v, FppC, Case No. 89-
0433.) Therefore, you may transfer funds frorn your city council
comrnittee to a committee you establish to run for mayor-.3
Section 85201 also requires that aI1 expenditures in connec-
tion with a particular election be made from the bank account
designated for that election. 
Funds held in a campaign bank ac-
count designated for your city council election should be
transferred to the bank account designated for the mayoral elec-
tion before being used for expenses issociated with tire rnayoral
election. 
This section also provides that aII contributions or
loans made to the candidate, to a per3on on behal-f of the
candidate, or to the candidate,s controlled committee shafl be
deposited in the account established for that office. 
Commission
regulations further state that:
When a contributor makes a contribution or loan to a
candidate with more than one controlled committee and does
not designate the cornmittee for which the contribution or
loan is made, the candidate or his or her designee nay aI-
Iocate the contribution or loan to any one of the candidate,s
controLled committees....
Regulation 18523 (a)
(copy enclosed) .
However, when soliciting funds, please note that Regulation
L8523.1 (copy enclosed) requires:
When a candidate or controlled committee of a candidate
makes_any written solicitation 
for contributions, the
soricitation sharr identify the particurar controrr-ed commit-
tee by.name, the specific office-and the particular election
for which contributions are sought, and sha11 instruct
contributors to designate their contributions tor ifrat
particular controlLed courmittee.
The contribution limits applY in the aggregate tO all cOmmit―
tees cOntrolled by the same~こ言

Addressing the issue of which funds may be used for recall
defense, expenses incurred by an officehotder who is the subject
of a recall fall into ts/o categories. First, they can be defined
as rrexpenses associated with holding officer as referred to in
Section 85202 (b) . In addition, they can be defined as expenses j,n
connection with a baLlot measure. Therefore, the funds from your
city council committee may be used to oppose the recal1. 
Ad-
ditional funds may be contributed to that account for the purpose
of the recall,. AII funds received in this account would be
subject to the contribution limits. 
In addition, once you have
been served with a notice of intention to circulate a recall peti-
tion and the notice has been published or posted in accordance
with Elections Code Section 27OO7 or other applicable law, a
separate cornmittee may be estabtished to raise contributions to
oppose the recall. 
Those contributions are not subject to the
contribution limitations. 
Holrever, tire funds nust be deposited in
an account separate from any other campaign account(s) and may not
be used for other election campaign or officeholder expenses.
(Hong Advice Letter, No. A-89-133; Roberti Advice Letter, No. A-
89-358, copies enclosed. )
If you have additional questions, please contact me at (91_6)
322-5662 .
S incerely ,
Kathryn E. Donovan
ceneral Counsel
A-90-202
Page 4
Enc I osures
By:  sandra L.

Taylor
Reform Consultant
